dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: Significantly improved: _BaseBM25 base class now contains all 25 shared methods. BM25/BM25L/BM25Plus/BM25T are thin ~50-line subclasses overriding only __init__, to_hybrid, and __reduce__. Remaining concern: HybridSearch still has some internal complexity that could be further decomposed.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Major improvement: search.py reduced from 1880 to ~750 lines. Type aliases consolidated into _types.py. search_query helper is properly shared. Remaining concern: search.py still contains BM25 classes + HybridSearch + Reranker together.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Architecture is now much cleaner: base class pattern eliminates massive duplication. _types.py centralises shared types. Remaining concern: HybridSearch and Reranker could be in separate modules for clearer separation.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: Greatly improved: duplicate _normalize_scores assignments fixed, _blend_reranked_scores extracted as standalone helper, no more copy-pasted methods across variants. _init_common helper avoids repetition.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Rust algorithms are well-optimized. Minor improvement: editops_multiword could hoist allocations outside per-char loop. Python code is clean with proper error handling.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Improved: _types.py centralises Result/MetaResult (was duplicated 3 times). engine.py and query.py now import from _types.py. Remaining concern: _search_query helper still duplicated in engine.py and search.py.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Some improvement: consolidated type aliases reduce inconsistency. Still uses Any for scorer, reranker, and embedding parameters where Protocol types would be more appropriate.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: Improved: from_column now consistently uses cls() pattern through base class. Still uses variant-specific return types instead of Self.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Improved: explain() now raises descriptive ValueError when document not found. assert statements in engine.py replaced with RuntimeError. Pattern is more consistent now.
dimension_notes.naming_quality: Mostly consistent. WRatio/QRatio PascalCase outliers remain for backward compatibility. _BaseBM25 naming follows Python conventions.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Base class pattern makes control flow much clearer. Each variant's unique logic is easily identifiable in thin __init__. _blend_reranked_scores helper improves readability.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: add_documents/remove_documents rebuilds remain but are now in one place (_rebuild_index). Cost documented implicitly through the pattern.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: WRatio/QRatio PascalCase and BM25Algorithm vs BM25Variant literal types remain. These are backward-compatibility concerns.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Clean optional dependency separation maintained. Lazy imports work correctly. Some exception chains still use 'from None'.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: All 534 tests pass. Still lacks parametrised tests for BM25 variant API parity, though this is less critical now with base class enforcing it.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: Improved with consolidated types. Document re-export from search.py maintained for backward compat. BM25Algorithm/BM25Variant literal inconsistency remains.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not directly applicable — library, not service. langchain.py lazy class pattern unchanged.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: Mostly resolved: _Result/_MetaResult consolidated into _types.py. query.py and engine.py now import from single source.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Improved: search.py halved in size. Could still benefit from splitting into search/ package but current size (~750 lines) is within bounds.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Reduced: the copy-paste pattern across BM25 variants (whether AI-generated or not) has been eliminated by the base class extraction.

issues.summary: Some parameters still use Any where Protocol types would be more specific
evidence: _BaseBM25._index typed as Any, reranker parameter uses Any, embedding callbacks use Any
issues.suggestion: Define Protocol classes for Scorer, Reranker, and EmbeddingCallback interfaces.

issues.summary: WRatio/QRatio use PascalCase while all other fuzz functions use snake_case
evidence: WRatio and QRatio alongside snake_case functions like partial_ratio, token_sort_ratio
issues.suggestion: Add snake_case aliases (w_ratio, q_ratio) for API consistency while keeping originals for backward compat.

issues.summary: No parametrised test verifies API parity across BM25 variants
evidence: BM25, BM25L, BM25Plus, BM25T should all support the same methods via _BaseBM25 but no test parametrises across variants
issues.suggestion: Add a parametrised test to verify all common methods exist and return correct results across variants.

issues.summary: _search_query helper still duplicated in search.py and engine.py
evidence: Both files define identical _search_query(owner) helper functions
issues.suggestion: Define _search_query once in search.py and import in engine.py, or move to a shared helpers module.

issues.summary: BM25Algorithm and BM25Variant literal types use different casing conventions
evidence: search.py: lowercase literals; join.py: PascalCase literals for the same concept
issues.suggestion: Consolidate into a single BM25Algorithm type with consistent casing.

issues.summary: _types.py uses underscore prefix which is correct but _BaseBM25 could use a cleaner public export
evidence: Result and MetaResult are public names but _types.py is private module
issues.suggestion: Consider whether Result/MetaResult should be in __all__ for public consumption.

issues.summary: search.py still contains BM25 + HybridSearch + Reranker in one module
evidence: ~750 lines with 3 distinct concerns: BM25 variants, HybridSearch, and Reranker
issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into search/bm25.py, search/hybrid.py, search/reranker.py in a future refactoring pass.

issues.summary: add_documents and remove_documents still rebuild the entire Rust index
evidence: _rebuild_index recreates the entire Rust index from scratch after each mutation
issues.suggestion: Document the rebuild cost in docstrings. Consider batch mutation APIs.

issues.summary: from_column uses _BaseBM25 return type instead of Self
evidence: _BaseBM25.from_column annotated as -> _BaseBM25 instead of -> Self
issues.suggestion: Use typing.Self (Python 3.11+) for from_column return type.

issues.summary: Some import error handlers use 'from None' losing original traceback
evidence: engine.py: 'raise ImportError(...) from None' discards original error context
issues.suggestion: Use 'from e' instead of 'from None' to preserve causal chain.

issues.summary: levenshtein_editops_multiword allocates vectors inside per-character loop
evidence: new_vp and new_vn allocated inside loop unlike myers_multiword which hoists and swaps
issues.suggestion: Hoist allocations and use std::mem::swap.

issues.summary: Scorer resolution logic repeated 4 times in process.py
evidence: Same scorer name/obj resolution pattern at lines 34-39, 82-84, 106-108, 139-141
issues.suggestion: Extract _resolve_scorer() helper function.

issues.summary: RuntimeError messages in engine.py could be more specific about initialization state
evidence: Generic 'Retriever has no index' message doesn't explain what went wrong
issues.suggestion: Include more context about which initialization step failed.

issues.summary: Document re-exported from search.py for backward compat when already exported from package
evidence: search.py line: 'from .document import Document  # noqa: F401'
issues.suggestion: Remove re-export if no external code imports Document from search module directly.

issues.summary: Minor remaining boilerplate in _init_common and __reduce__ methods
evidence: _init_common helper and __reduce__ contain somewhat formulaic code across variants
issues.suggestion: This is inherent to the pattern and acceptable; no further refactoring needed.

issues.summary: langchain.py uses module __getattr__ for lazy class construction
evidence: Module-level __getattr__ dynamically builds RustfuzzBM25Retriever class
issues.suggestion: Document the pattern or use simpler lazy import approach.

issues.summary: _Result and _MetaResult backward-compat aliases remain in search.py
evidence: search.py keeps _Result = Result and _MetaResult = MetaResult aliases
issues.suggestion: Plan deprecation cycle for _Result/_MetaResult in favor of Result/MetaResult from _types.

issues.summary: HybridSearch and Reranker in same module as BM25 classes
evidence: BM25 variants, HybridSearch, and Reranker all in search.py despite different concerns
issues.suggestion: Future split into sub-package would further improve architecture clarity.

issues.summary: Reranker.__init__ has a chain of hasattr checks for auto-detecting score interface
evidence: Reranker checks predict, compute_scores, score, callable in sequence
issues.suggestion: Consider using a Protocol/ABC for the scoring interface instead of duck-typing at runtime.

issues.summary: _coerce_corpus helper includes Document/LangChain handling that duplicates compat.py logic
evidence: _coerce_corpus handles Document, LangChain Document, and plain strings separately
issues.suggestion: Consider merging with _coerce_to_strings or documenting the distinct purposes more clearly.
